title: Coronation in Kent
title_candidates: Wire to Wire on the Channel
theme: Jon Baker's wire-to-wire coronation: a record-low Gross, a Trophy never relinquished, and a Kent debut that became a procession — set against Williams's bridesmaid week, Patterson's split personality, and Mullin's third Spoon in three TEGs.
opening_hook: Open on the final scoreboard at Prince's: Jon Baker +50 gross, winning the Jacket by 18 — the lowest Gross total in TEG history — and the Trophy by 6. State the headline result up front, then walk it back to Royal Cinque Ports and the 9-shot cushion that was there by tea on Day 1.
foreshadow: Jon Baker arrived on back-to-back Jacket wins, hunting a three-peat
David Mullin arrived as reigning Spoon holder with back-to-back Spoons already on the card
Defending Trophy champion John Patterson opened on Royal Cinque Ports with an 8 at the 1st
Gregg Williams came in with three prior Trophy wins but second in two of the last three TEGs — the bridesmaid label needed shaking

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Led from R1 H1 to R4 H18. Williams drew level at R2 H15; Jon birdied the 18th the same afternoon (b35) to reclaim outright and was never headed in stroke terms again. Williams drew level once more at R3 H1 (b48), but Jon's +8 gross / 44-pt R3 at Littlestone (b03) reopened a 5-shot cushion that grew to 6 by the close. Final: 158 pts, by 6.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A procession. Nine clear after R1 (+13 at Royal Cinque Ports), seven after R2, fourteen after a +8 R3 at Littlestone — the 2nd-best Gross round in TEG history to date — and eighteen at the finish. Final: +50 gross, the lowest total in TEG history and Jon's third Jacket.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Six bottom-place changes on Day 1 alone before settling; Patterson and Alex Baker held the basement at various points through R3. Mullin sat 3rd or 4th most of the week, then ran a 9 at the 10th in R4 — par 4, Stroke Index 3 — and dropped to last on the spot (b10, b12). Final: 135 pts, his third Wooden Spoon and third in three TEGs.

headline_candidates: Day One on the Kent Coast: Jon +13, Everyone Else +Lots
rounds.angle: Jon Baker opens with 39 pts and +13 gross, nine clear on the Jacket and six on the Trophy after a single round — while Patterson stacks four quadruple-or-worse holes and the basement of the Spoon race changes hands six times.

At Prince's Dunes/Himalayas, Williams claws level on the Trophy at H15 — the only sustained challenge of the week — before Jon birdies the last (b35) to reclaim outright. Patterson bleeds 11 in the first four holes; Mullin posts an 8 at the 9th.

headline_candidates: Littlestone: Two Records in One Afternoon
Jon +8, Patterson 49 — On the Same Card
rounds.chosen_headline: Littlestone: Two Records in One Afternoon
rounds.angle: The week's magic round. Jon Baker shoots +8 gross — the 2nd-best Gross round in TEG history (b03). Defending Trophy champion John Patterson — author of multiple quadruples already — posts 49 pts and a personal-best +12 gross (b05, b06), the 3rd-best Stableford round ever. Williams draws level for an hour at H1, then watches the door shut.

headline_candidates: Mullin's 9 on SI 3, and the Spoon Is Sealed
rounds.angle: Jon closes out both competitions on the Shore/Dunes; Patterson eagles the par-5 2nd (b02) for the field's only eagle of the week; Alex Baker posts a PB +17 gross even as he finishes the week tied to the floor of the field; Mullin's quintuple-bogey 9 at the 10th — par 4, SI 3 — confirms a third consecutive Spoon.

players.arc: Coronation. Led both competitions wire-to-wire across four rounds, set the lowest Gross total in TEG history (+50, by 18), claimed a third Trophy and a third Jacket in succession — the kind of week the others will need a year to stop talking about.

The bridesmaid week extended. Drew level for the Trophy twice (R2 H15, R3 H1) and led neither outright for a single hole, finishing 2nd in both Trophy and Jacket — a fourth runner-up finish in five TEGs.

Jekyll and Hyde from the defending Trophy champion: an 8 at the 1st of Royal Cinque Ports, four quadruples in R1 alone, another two on Day 2 — and then at Littlestone, the eagle's-shadow 49 pts and a personal-best gross. The eagle itself came at Prince's the next day. Finished mid-pack despite the catalogue of disasters.

Reigning Spoon holder collects another. Three Wooden Spoons in three TEGs, four overall — the R4 H10 quintuple bogey on the third-hardest hole on the course was the moment it was sealed. The eight Jackets feel a long way away.
A tough week that ended with a personal-best Gross round (+17 at Prince's Shore/Dunes) and a tour of the Spoon basement before Mullin took it off him. Quadruples at R1 H11 and R3 H2 the lowlights.

venue_notes: TEG's first visit to Kent — and a first visit to all four courses. Frame the week as a links pilgrimage on the Channel coast: Royal Cinque Ports (Deal, Open venue) as the brutal opening test; Prince's (Sandwich, Open venue, 2030 Walker Cup host) hosting both Day 2 and the closing day in different routings; Littlestone on the Romney Marshes (Purves, refined by Braid and MacKenzie) as the unlikely stage for the week's two record-class rounds. No prior course history exists — every shot is a debut shot — so lean on the architecture and the wind rather than ghosts of TEGs past.
course_history_notes: All four Kent courses were new to TEG; every player's scorecard at every venue is a personal debut, so no course PBs are 'broken' here — the records that matter are TEG-wide (b01, b03, b05).
decisive_moments: Trophy: R2 H18 at Prince's — Jon Baker's birdie reclaiming the outright lead from a level-pegged Williams, the last time anyone got within touching distance.
Green Jacket: R1 at Royal Cinque Ports — the +13 / 9-shot cushion that turned the Jacket race into a four-day procession before it had really begun.
Wooden Spoon: R4 H10 at Prince's Shore/Dunes — David Mullin's quintuple-bogey 9 on SI 3 dropping him to the bottom of the field with eight holes to play, and no time to climb back.

payoffs.seed: Jon Baker's back-to-back Jackets going in
payoffs.payoff: Three in a row, and not just three — the lowest Gross total in TEG history (+50), by 18 shots.
Make it three. The R4 H10 9 on SI 3 confirmed it; 135 pts, last.

payoffs.seed: Patterson, defending Trophy champion, opens with an 8 at the 1st
payoffs.payoff: The opening-hole disaster set the tone for the catalogue (five quadruples or worse across R1–R4) — and yet at Littlestone the same player posts 49 pts and a personal-best gross, the 3rd-best Stableford round in TEG history.

payoffs.resolves_in: How the three were decided / closing summary
payoffs.payoff: Drew level twice on the Trophy, led outright for zero holes, finished 2nd in both competitions. The label stays on.
